title: Drosera monantha
intro: Drosera monantha, commonly known as the single-petaled sundew, is a carnivorous plant species native to Australia and Tasmania, belonging to the Droseraceae family. The health benefits of Drosera monantha include its potential to aid in respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to relieve congestion. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, specifically secologanin and loganin, as well as other alkaloids and flavonoids. Traditionally, Drosera monantha is used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures, teas, and syrups, often used to treat respiratory complaints and other conditions.
taxonomy: Drosera monantha belongs to the family Droseraceae, commonly known as the sundew family. This carnivorous plant is classified as a genus of Drosera, which comprises around 193 species. Drosera monantha is further categorized into the section Atractocaulis of the genus Drosera. The species is native to Australia and is found in Western Australia. The classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and its role as a carnivorous plant in its native habitat.
